title: Implement checkout service with cart and inventory management
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_new:
from decimal import Decimal
from flask import g, request
from app.models import Cart, EmailLog, Inventory, Order, db


def process_checkout() -> dict:
    user_id = request.json["user_id"]
    cart_items = db.session.query(Cart).filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()
    total = Decimal("0")
    order_lines = []
    for item in cart_items:
        product = db.session.query(Inventory).filter_by(sku=item.sku).first()
        if not product or product.stock < item.quantity:
            return {"error": f"Out of stock: {item.sku}"}
        product.stock -= item.quantity
        line_total = Decimal(str(product.price)) * item.quantity
        total += line_total
        order_lines.append(
            {"sku": item.sku, "qty": item.quantity, "price": str(product.price)}
        )
    order = Order(user_id=user_id, total=str(total), lines=order_lines)
    db.session.add(order)
    db.session.flush()
    email_body = f"Thank you for your order #{order.id}. Total: ${total}"
    db.session.add(EmailLog(to=g.user.email, subject="Order Confirmation", body=email_body))
    db.session.commit()
    return {"order_id": str(order.id), "total": str(total)}

expected_findings.title: Flask request object accessed directly inside the service layer
expected_findings.rationale: The service function reads from Flask's HTTP request context, coupling business logic to the web framework. Services should receive plain arguments so they remain independently testable outside a request cycle.

expected_findings.title: Email notification concern embedded in the checkout service
expected_findings.rationale: The checkout service constructs and persists an EmailLog record, violating single-responsibility. Email notification belongs in a separate notification service invoked by the caller after checkout succeeds.
